details.body: finding_summary: `git_diff_fingerprint` hashes `git status --porcelain` output instead of the actual diff, so further edits to a file that is already dirty leave the fingerprint unchanged (` M path` / `?? path`). In `iterative_minimal`, that makes `diff_changed` go false even while the implementer is still changing contents, so the loop can hit the stable exit early. affected_files: src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5911-5946, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6079-6081. remediation_hint: fingerprint real content-level state (for example staged diff + unstaged diff + untracked file contents, or a full workspace content hash) instead of `git status` category lines.

finding_summary: The iterative loop's `iteration` and `stable_count` live only in local variables, so they reset whenever the stage is retried or the run is resumed. `resume_iteration_counters` restores only QA/review counters, and `ActiveRun` has nowhere to persist iterative loop state. As a result, an interrupted or retryable `plan_and_implement` attempt can consume another full `max_consecutive_implementer_rounds` budget and restart from iteration 1, violating the configured cap and stable-window semantics. affected_files: src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:161-179, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1096-1108, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5551-5756, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5975-5988. remediation_hint: persist iterative loop state in `ActiveRun` (at least current iteration and current stable streak) and resume/retry from that state instead of reinitializing the loop.

`git_diff_fingerprint` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5911-5946` uses `git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all`, whose output is identical across different content modifications to a file that is already modified or already untracked. Experimentally verified: editing an already-`?? newfile.txt` file to different bytes, or an already-` M file.txt` file to different bytes, yields the same porcelain string before and after. This means `diff_before != diff_after` returns `false` for iterations that change only already-dirty files, so the stability counter (`advance_iterative_loop_state`) increments when the agent is still making real changes, and the loop can exit with reason=`stable` prematurely. The existing test `iterative_minimal_loops_plan_and_implement_until_stable_then_runs_final_review` does not catch this because its stub only creates a new file once (transition from clean) and never modifies an already-dirty file across iterations. Fix: switch to a fingerprint that captures working-tree content — e.g. combine `git diff HEAD -- . :(exclude).ralph-burning` (for tracked, state + content) with content hashes of untracked files from `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -- . :(exclude).ralph-burning`, or reuse the existing `hash_workspace_entries` logic but restrict it to paths reported by git. Also add a regression test that modifies an already-dirty file across iterations to verify diff_changed stays `true`.

`is_non_git_workspace_error` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5850-5854` matches git's stderr against the English strings "not a git repository", "unable to find repository", and "failed to discover repository". `git_diff_fingerprint` invokes git with the inherited environment, so when a user runs in a non-English locale that has git translations installed (`LANG=de_DE.UTF-8`, `LC_ALL=fr_FR.UTF-8`, etc.), the stderr will be translated and the substring match will fail. The function then returns `AppError::StageCommitFailed` instead of falling back to `workspace_diff_fingerprint`, so iterative_minimal runs outside git (or in a locale where git localizes fatal messages) will fail the stage rather than using the filesystem fingerprint. Fix: set a C-locale environment on the `Command` (e.g. `.env("LC_ALL", "C").env("LANG", "C").env_remove("LANGUAGE")`) before calling `.output()`, and/or detect the condition by checking for the presence of `.git` or running `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` up front instead of parsing stderr text.
